title: Finding Files Across the Linux Filesystem
scenario: You have just joined the operations team and received a ticket about several missing files scattered across a Linux server. A custom application configuration file exists somewhere under /etc, an orphaned log file larger than 50 KB exists somewhere in /var/log, and a helper shell script exists somewhere under /opt. The location of the Bash executable must also be identified for a deployment script that is being written.
symptoms: Custom application config file missing from known location
Orphaned log file larger than 50 KB exists somewhere in /var/log
Helper shell script lost somewhere under /opt
Deployment script needs the exact path of the bash executable
mission: Use 'find' and 'which' to locate the target files and save their absolute paths to ~/found_config.txt, ~/found_largelog.txt, ~/found_script.txt, and ~/bash_location.txt.

hints.title: Searching by name
hints.content: 'find' takes a starting directory and a -name option. Think about how to structure a find command that searches under /etc for a file with a specific name.

hints.title: Filtering by size
hints.content: 'find' supports a -size flag. Sizes can be expressed in kilobytes with the 'k' suffix. A '+' before the number means larger than.

hints.title: Saving output
hints.content: Use '>' to redirect the output of any command into a file. For example: some_command > ~/output.txt

hints.title: Finding executables
hints.content: There is a dedicated command for locating where an executable lives on the system. It takes the name of the command as its argument.

hints.title: Bonus
hints.content: Try using 'locate custom_app.conf' to find the same config file using the system's file index. You may need to run 'updatedb' first to refresh the index. Notice how much faster 'locate' is compared to 'find'.

acceptance_criteria.description: /home/intern/found_config.txt contains the absolute path to custom_app.conf
/home/intern/found_largelog.txt contains the absolute path to the orphaned log file
/home/intern/found_script.txt contains the absolute path to helper.sh
/home/intern/bash_location.txt contains the absolute path to the bash executable
